Shop.title: Shop
Shop.description: Represents a single shop operated by a shopkeeper, containing essential business details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Shop entity.

shopkeeperId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the shopkeeper (user) who owns this shop, provided by an external authentication system. (Relationship: Shopkeeper 1:1 Shop)

name.description: The official name of the shop.

address.description: The physical address of the shop.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the shop.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the shop, distinct from the shopkeeper's login email.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product available in the shop, including its inventory details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

shopId.description: Reference to the Shop this product belongs to. (Relationship: Shop 1:N Product)

category.description: The category the product belongs to (e.g., 'Dairy', 'Snacks').

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

quantity.description: The current stock level (inventory quantity) of the product.

barcode.description: The barcode identifier for the product, used for quick scanning and unique within a shop.

lowStockThreshold.description: The minimum quantity level at which the product is considered low in stock and should trigger an alert.

Sale.title: Sale
Sale.description: Represents a single sales transaction recorded in the shop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sale entity.

shopId.description: Reference to the Shop where this sale occurred. (Relationship: Shop 1:N Sale)

saleDateTime.description: The date and time when the sale was recorded.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the sale.

SaleItem.title: SaleItem
SaleItem.description: Represents a single product item sold as part of a larger sales transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SaleItem entity.

saleId.description: Reference to the Sale this item is part of. (Relationship: Sale 1:N SaleItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was sold in this sale item. (Relationship: Product 1:N SaleItem)

quantitySold.description: The quantity of the product sold in this particular sale item.

priceAtSale.description: The price of the product at the time of this specific sale item (important for historical accuracy, as product prices can change over time).

Alert.title: Alert
Alert.description: Represents a proactive notification or warning about product stock levels or predicted run-outs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity.

shopId.description: Reference to the Shop this alert is associated with. (Relationship: Shop 1:N Alert)

productId.description: Reference to the Product this alert pertains to. (Relationship: Product 1:N Alert)

type.description: The type of alert, e.g., 'low_stock' or 'predicted_run_out'.

message.description: A descriptive message detailing the alert.

alertDateTime.description: The date and time when the alert was generated.

isDismissed.description: Indicates whether the shopkeeper has dismissed this alert.

predictedRunOutDays.description: The estimated number of days until the product is predicted to run out of stock. Applicable for 'predicted_run_out' type alerts, otherwise null.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all shops. Each shop document is owned by a specific shopkeeper and includes the 'shopkeeperId' field for direct authorization checks.

Subcollection for products belonging to a specific shop. Each product document includes the 'shopkeeperId' denormalized from its parent shop for authorization independence, allowing direct security rule checks.
Subcollection for sales transactions recorded in a specific shop. Each sale document includes the 'shopkeeperId' denormalized from its parent shop for authorization independence.

params.name: saleId
params.description: Unique identifier for the sales transaction.

definition.description: Subcollection for individual items within a sales transaction. Each sale item document includes the 'shopkeeperId' denormalized from its grandparent shop for authorization independence, enabling robust security rules without 'get()' calls.

params.name: saleId
params.description: Unique identifier for the sales transaction.

params.name: saleItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the individual item within the sales transaction.

definition.description: Subcollection for alerts related to a specific shop. Each alert document includes the 'shopkeeperId' denormalized from its parent shop for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for KiranaLink is designed around the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, prioritizing security and debuggability. All entities are organized hierarchically under a `/shops/{shopId}` root, reflecting the app's shop-centric data model.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which can break atomic operations and complicate debugging, the `shopkeeperId` from the `Shop` entity is denormalized and stored directly within all subcollection documents (Product, Sale, SaleItem, Alert). This ensures that any document, regardless of its depth in the hierarchy, contains the necessary authorization context (`shopkeeperId`) to determine if `request.auth.uid` has access. For example, a `Product` document will contain its `shopId` (from the path) and its `shopkeeperId` (denormalized from the parent shop), allowing rules to simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.shopkeeperId`.

**Query as Policy (QAPs) Support:**
1.  **`/shops` Collection:** Shopkeepers can list their owned shops by querying `shops` where `shopkeeperId == request.auth.uid`. The security rule for this collection will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `shopkeeperId` field on any `Shop` document. This is a secure `list` operation, as documents are implicitly filtered by ownership.
2.  **Subcollections (e.g., `/shops/{shopId}/products`):** The hierarchical path structure naturally scopes data to a specific `shopId`. By denormalizing the `shopkeeperId` into each `Product` document, security rules can confirm that `request.auth.uid` is the owner of the `shopId` specified in the path AND matches the `shopkeeperId` stored within the `Product` document itself. This dual check prevents users from accessing subcollections of shops they don't own by simply guessing `shopId`s in the path. All `list` operations within these subcollections are secure because the rule ensures that any document returned by a query belongs to the authenticated user's shop.

**Structural Segregation:**
Each collection (e.g., `/shops`, `/shops/{shopId}/products`) contains documents with a homogeneous security posture. All products within a shop's `products` subcollection are accessible/modifiable by the same `shopkeeperId`. This avoids complex conditional logic in security rules, making them simpler and more robust.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data & Hierarchical Paths:** The entire structure follows a clear hierarchical path for user-owned data (`/shops/{shopId}/products/{productId}`). This makes ownership clear and security rules straightforward. The `shopkeeperId` acts as the primary access control mechanism.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
Wildcards like `{shopId}` and `{productId}` are semantically clear. Authorization fields are standardized to `shopkeeperId`. This consistency aids development and debugging.
